title: Tender Energizing Ethiopian Fenugreek Dulet
description: A traditional Ethiopian fermented dish that's high in protein and fiber, perfect for breakfast or lunch. Fenugreek seeds and lamination puff add a unique flavor and texture.
instructions: Combine fenugreek seeds and water in a bowl and let it ferment for 24 hours at room temperature.
Strain the liquid and discard the solids.
Cut the lamination puff into small squares and soak them in water for 30 minutes.
Drain the lamination puff and grind them into a fine paste using a blender or food processor.
Heat oil in a pan over medium heat and sauté the chopped onion until it's translucent.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger and sauté for another minute.
Add the chickpeas, cumin, coriander, turmeric, and salt. Cook for 5 minutes.
Add the fenugreek liquid and cook for another 10 minutes.
Add the lamination puff paste and stir well.
Cook for another 5 minutes and adjust the seasoning as needed.
Garnish with chopped microgreens if using.
Serve warm or at room temperature.
Refrigerate any leftovers for up to 3 days and reheat before serving.
